claimText: Sundarbans Tiger Reserve is India's second-largest tiger reserve, behind Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam Tiger Reserve.
evidenceExcerpt: Now India's second-largest tiger reserve, behind Nagarjunasagar-Srisailam.
examUse: Statement-based question on the largest and second-largest tiger reserves in India.
distractorHooks: Claiming Sundarbans is the largest tiger reserve in India
Naming a different reserve like Jim Corbett or Bandipur as the largest

claimText: Sundarbans is the only mangrove forest in the world that houses a significant tiger population.
evidenceExcerpt: The only mangrove forest in the world housing a significant tiger population.
examUse: Statement-based question on unique habitats of tigers globally.
distractorHooks: Claiming other mangrove forests like Bhitarkanika also have significant tiger populations
Stating tigers are not found in mangrove ecosystems

claimText: Sundarbans Tiger Reserve is bordered by the River Matla to the west and Rivers Bidya and Gomdi to the north-west.
evidenceExcerpt: Bordered by the River Matla to the west and Rivers Bidya & Gomdi to the north-west.
examUse: Correct-pair or map-based question matching rivers to protected areas.
distractorHooks: Swapping the rivers with other major rivers like Hooghly or Teesta
Changing the cardinal directions of the bordering rivers

claimText: Sundarbans Tiger Reserve holds multiple international designations including UNESCO World Heritage Site, CA|TS, and MAB Programme site.
evidenceExcerpt: Holds designations as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, CA|TS, and MAB Programme site.
examUse: Multiple-statement question regarding the international recognition status of specific tiger reserves.
distractorHooks: Stating it is not recognized under the MAB Programme
